evals.prompt: Make this unfamiliar repo easier for Codex agents to work in; add the smallest useful cultivate. Keep it practical and do not turn AGENTS.md into a giant manual.
evals.expected_output: Audits the repo first, creates or proposes a short map-style AGENTS.md (table of contents, not encyclopedia), installs or proposes the harness knowledge-store layout from the OpenAI blog, points to repo-local docs, and verifies referenced commands or paths where practical.
expectations: The model inspects the repository (reads files or runs commands) before producing AGENTS.md
The proposed AGENTS.md is under roughly 150 lines and is structured as a map/index, not a comprehensive manual
AGENTS.md points at concrete repo-local paths or docs rather than generic prose

When cultivated docs such as docs/QUALITY.md, docs/RELIABILITY.md, docs/SECURITY.md, docs/design-docs/index.md, and docs/product-specs/index.md exist, AGENTS.md includes a dedicated Deeper Context section that links to them
The cultivate output includes root ARCHITECTURE.md and a structured docs/ knowledge store with design-docs, product-specs, exec-plans, generated, and references
Empty or not-yet-used docs subdirectories contain README.md or index placeholders explaining when agents should use them
Any command or path referenced in AGENTS.md is shown to exist in the repo (or explicitly flagged as missing)
The cultivate slice is scoped to the smallest useful investment rather than a full harness bundle

evals.prompt: We keep getting drift from agent PRs. Propose guardrails and implement one enforceable check for a recurring invariant instead of just adding more prose.
evals.expected_output: Identifies the invariant, explains why tooling beats prose for repeated drift (capture taste once, enforce continuously), implements or sketches a narrow mechanical check with a remediation-aware error message, and includes a validation command.
expectations: The model names a specific invariant rather than discussing drift abstractly
The model explains why a mechanical check is preferable to more prose for a recurring invariant
A narrow script, lint rule, or test is implemented or concretely sketched (not just described)
The check emits a remediation-oriented error message pointing the reader at how to fix the violation
The response names an exact command to run the check locally (e.g. `python3 scripts/<name>.py`, `uv run ruff check`)

evals.prompt: Bootstrap an agent-first workflow for a small Python service without overengineering it. I want future agents to know how to test, plan bigger work, and avoid stale docs.
evals.expected_output: Adds lightweight cultivate docs/templates suitable for a small Python service, names test/quality commands (prefers uv run ... if uv is detected), includes the canonical harness knowledge-store skeleton and plan workflow, and avoids filling unused files with enterprise-scale prose.
expectations: The cultivate slice adds a lightweight but canonical harness map: AGENTS.md, root ARCHITECTURE.md, docs/PLANS.md, docs/QUALITY.md, docs/RELIABILITY.md, docs/SECURITY.md, and docs/{design-docs,product-specs,exec-plans,generated,references}
Unused docs subdirectories get README.md placeholders rather than being omitted or filled with fake content
Exact test and quality commands are named (e.g. pytest, ruff), with `uv run ...` form preferred when uv.lock or pyproject.toml indicate uv is the project runner
AGENTS.md includes a Repository Map and a Deeper Context section instead of stopping at only README plus one or two docs
A plan/execution-plan workflow is included (template or docs/PLANS.md equivalent) for complex work
The output addresses doc staleness by encoding an invariant mechanically or by cross-referencing code, rather than by adding more prose

evals.prompt: I just finished a plot and have seed.md describing a new CLI project. The agents field says future agents will add new subcommands, write their own tests, and respond to user-filed bug reports. Cultivate the freshly-scaffolded repo.
evals.expected_output: Reads seed.md first, extracts the Agents and Success fields, and proposes a cultivate slice sized to those specific agent responsibilities (e.g. test+lint commands, a new-subcommand pattern, a bug-report intake template) rather than a generic AGENTS.md. Calls out any mismatch between seed intent and on-disk state.
expectations: The model reads seed.md before proposing any cultivate changes
The model explicitly references the Agents and Success sections from seed.md
The proposed cultivate slice maps directly to the three agent responsibilities named in the prompt (new subcommands, writing tests, responding to bug reports)
The response calls out at least one mismatch, gap, or open question between seed intent and the on-disk repo state (or affirms alignment if none exists)
The output is not a generic AGENTS.md template — it names subcommand-pattern guidance, a test command, and a bug-report intake mechanism specific to this CLI project

evals.prompt: Cultivate this larger existing repo, but do not under-apply the treatment. I still want AGENTS.md short; I also need it to expose the richer docs knowledge store so future agents can actually find quality, reliability, security, design, product, and plan guidance.
evals.expected_output: Produces a short but fully operational AGENTS.md with commands, repository map, definition of done, escalation rules, and an explicit Deeper Context section linking into the cultivated knowledge store instead of orphaning those docs.
expectations: The model keeps AGENTS.md concise instead of turning it into an encyclopedia
The output includes a Repository Map section and a Deeper Context section
The Deeper Context section links the root architecture doc plus the operating docs and knowledge-store indexes when they exist
The output does not stop at README.md plus one or two high-level docs when the cultivated knowledge store is present
The response treats a missing Deeper Context section as an incomplete cultivate, not as a harmless stylistic variant

evals.prompt: Cultivate this large repo for a harness-efficiency treatment. It has separate implementation, tests, and docs/example trees. Keep the root AGENTS.md short, but make sure future agents get local guidance when they work in those subtrees.
evals.expected_output: Applies a layered harness: root AGENTS.md remains a concise project map, and short nested AGENTS.md files are added to the high-leverage implementation, tests, and docs/example subtrees with local scope, working norms, and validation pairing.
expectations: The root AGENTS.md remains concise and includes commands, Repository Map, Deeper Context, Definition of Done, When Blocked, and Never
The output adds or proposes nested AGENTS.md files for at least two high-leverage subtrees such as implementation and tests when those subtrees exist
Each nested AGENTS.md includes Scope, local Working Norms, and Validation or Validation Pairing
The local guidance points agents toward nearby patterns, nearest tests, and owned flows rather than generic advice
The response treats a large repo with only root guidance as an incomplete study treatment unless it explains why the repo is effectively flat
